title: These 8 Stocks Yield Up to 8.3% and Their Payouts Could Soon Rise
author: Brett Owens
description: The article highlights eight dividend-paying stocks with yields up to 8.3% that are expected to increase their payouts soon. These companies have demonstrated strong earnings growth and maintain low payout ratios, suggesting room for dividend increases. The stocks span various sectors including construction, healthcare, aerospace, HVAC, telecommunications, tobacco, investment management, and energy infrastructure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Dividend has doubled in three years with 33.3% boost last year; expected continued bottom-line growth; dividend announcement expected mid-September
Two-decade uninterrupted dividend increases; doubled payout over five years; 20% increase in 2025 despite profit pullback; double-digit profit rebound expected; announcement early August
23% net income growth in 2025; 33% growth expected in 2026; pays out less than 10% of earnings; likely summer dividend hike announcement
Net income nearly doubled in 2025; dividend 77.7% higher year-over-year; multiple raises per year since 2021; strong growth expected; announcement late July

title: 3 Stocks Cashing In on AI While Everyone Watches NVIDIA
author: Bridget Bennett
description: While investors focus on semiconductor giants like NVIDIA, infrastructure companies supporting AI data center buildout are delivering stronger earnings and better value. Marc Chaikin identifies three stocks—Argan, MasTec, and Quanta Services—that are positioned to benefit from the multi-year expansion of power plants, electrical grids, and construction needed for AI infrastructure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as the focus of investor attention but noted as underperforming with less than 1% gains year-to-date, suggesting the major gains have already been realized in the semiconductor space.

Positioned as a construction and engineering firm with strong fundamentals, multi-year growth runway in power plant development, and recent pullback offering entry opportunity. Retail investors haven't discovered it yet, suggesting upside potential.

Demonstrated strong earnings beat (41 cents above estimate), raised guidance, shows consistent positive earnings surprises over six quarters, analyst estimate revisions, and ratings upgrades. Recent 20% pullback viewed as buying opportunity.

Largest company on list with $30B revenue, posted 35% earnings beat, positive surprises in 18 of last 20 quarters, 15% spike on recent report. Exposure extends beyond AI to aging U.S. electrical infrastructure with multi-year durability.
Mentioned as having already posted blowout earnings and doubled in value, suggesting the major gains in the hardware/server layer have already been realized.
Grouped with NVIDIA and Micron as semiconductor plays that have already had their run, with biggest gains already realized.
Included in semiconductor category where major gains have already been realized according to the article's thesis.

title: The Argan Stock Rally Still Isn't Over, Even After the Stock Has Nearly Doubled This Year
author: Marc Guberti
description: Argan, a power generation and infrastructure specialist, has nearly doubled in value this year driven by surging demand for electricity to power AI data centers. The company more than doubled its backlog to $2.9 billion in fiscal 2026, with 50% year-over-year revenue growth. Analysts expect the AI infrastructure boom to sustain beyond 2030, positioning Argan for continued strong growth as data center electricity demand is projected to nearly triple by 2030.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company has nearly doubled year-to-date with backlog more than doubling to $2.9 billion, 50% YoY revenue growth, zero debt, and positioned to benefit from multi-year AI infrastructure buildout with electricity demand projected to nearly triple by 2030.

title: Earnings Volatility Watch: 10 Stocks Set For Big Moves This Week, From Nuclear Power To Whiskey Maker
author: Piero Cingari
description: Options markets are pricing significant implied moves between 15.92% and 21.71% across 10 stocks reporting this week, spanning AI infrastructure suppliers, nuclear power, cybersecurity, and consumer staples. The largest moves are expected for Netskope (21.71%), Brown-Forman (20.51%), and Planet Labs (18.95%), with major mega-cap companies like Broadcom, CrowdStrike, and Palo Alto Networks also reporting amid continued AI infrastructure demand.

title: Forget Tech: These 3 Funds Yield 11% and They’re Just Getting Started
author: Brett Owens
description: As tech stocks dominate market gains, contrarian investors can capitalize on discounted closed-end funds offering yields up to 11.8%. Three funds—Gabelli Equity Trust (GAB), DoubleLine Income Solutions Fund (DSL), and NXG Nextgen Infrastructure Income Fund (NXG)—provide diversified exposure to stocks, bonds, and infrastructure while trading at significant discounts to net asset value.
